name: Kena Upanishad
summary: The Kena Upanishad asks 'By whom?' — by whose will does the mind think, the eye see, the ear hear? It teaches that Brahman is the unseen Seer, the unheard Hearer — the ground of all knowing, itself unknown by the ordinary mind.

verses.sanskrit:
केनेषितं पतति प्रेषितं मनः केन प्राणः प्रथमः प्रैति युक्तः |
केनेषितां वाचमिमां वदन्ति चक्षुः श्रोत्रं क उ देवो युनक्ति ||१||
verses.transliteration:
keneṣitaṃ patati preṣitaṃ manaḥ kena prāṇaḥ prathamaḥ praiti yuktaḥ |
keneṣitāṃ vācam imāṃ vadanti cakṣuḥ śrotraṃ ka u devo yunakti ||1||
verses.english_translation: Willed by whom does the directed mind fall upon its objects? By whom is the first vital breath set in motion? By whose will do people utter this speech? What god directs the eye and ear?
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: केन — कस्य प्रेरणया — मनः इषितम् — प्रेरितम् — विषयेषु पतति। प्राणः प्रथमः — मुख्यप्राणः — केन युक्तः प्रैति।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: By whose impulsion does the mind fall on its objects? By whom is the chief prana set in motion? The student asks about the ultimate ground of all cognitive and vital activity.

verses.sanskrit:
श्रोत्रस्य श्रोत्रं मनसो मनो यद्वाचो ह वाचं स उ प्राणस्य प्राणः |
चक्षुषश्चक्षुरतिमुच्य धीराः प्रेत्यास्माल्लोकादमृता भवन्ति ||२||
verses.transliteration:
śrotrasya śrotraṃ manaso mano yad vāco ha vācaṃ sa u prāṇasya prāṇaḥ |
cakṣuṣaś cakṣur ati mucya dhīrāḥ pretyāsmāl lokād amṛtā bhavanti ||2||
verses.english_translation: It is the ear of the ear, the mind of the mind, the speech of speech, the life of life, the eye of the eye. The wise, departing from this world, become immortal.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: श्रोत्रस्य श्रोत्रम् — श्रोत्रं प्रकाशयति यत् तत् ब्रह्म श्रोत्रस्य श्रोत्रम्। तत् ब्रह्म न चक्षुषा न मनसा गृह्यते।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: That which illumines the ear is the 'ear of the ear' — Brahman. It cannot be grasped by the eye or mind, for it is their very ground. The wise who know this become immortal.
